items.title: USDA final rule updates SNAP retailer stocking standards; enforcement begins this fall
items.summary: USDA finalized new SNAP retailer stocking standards that require authorized stores to carry 7 varieties in each of 4 staple food categories (up from 3) and to stock at least 1 perishable variety in 3 categories (up from 2). The rule is effective July 7, 2026, and retailers must comply no later than Nov. 4, 2026.

items.title: CMS guidance: limits on federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens start Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS issued a State Health Official letter to prepare states for a statutory change that limits claiming federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for individuals who are not U.S. citizens/nationals or who fall into certain noncitizen categories identified in law. CMS says states must implement the change beginning Oct. 1, 2026, while noting exceptions such as federal matching for emergency medical conditions and states’ options to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant people.

items.title: SSA posts 2026 SSI federal payment amounts after 2.8% COLA
items.summary: SSA lists the 2026 maximum federal SSI payment as $994 per month for an eligible individual and $1,491 per month for an eligible individual with an eligible spouse, with $498 per month for an essential person. SSA notes the 2.8% cost-of-living increase is effective January 2026.

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC fruit-and-vegetable cash-value benefit at $26 (kids) and $48–$52 (adults)
items.summary: USDA FNS issued WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 setting FY 2026 monthly cash-value benefit amounts at $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully or mostly breastfeeding participants. The memo says FY 2026 runs from Oct. 1, 2025, through Sept. 30, 2026, and notes the $48 amount is a $1 increase over FY 2025.

items.title: FAFSA: federal deadline for 2025–26 aid year is June 30, 2026
items.summary: USA.gov reminds students the federal FAFSA deadline for the 2025–26 school year is June 30, 2026 (and June 30, 2027 for 2026–27). The page also notes that many states have their own FAFSA deadlines, so applicants should check state requirements.

items.title: IRS updates EITC tables for tax year 2025: maximum credit up to $8,046
items.summary: IRS EITC tables show the maximum Earned Income Tax Credit for tax year 2025 ranges from $649 (no qualifying children) to $8,046 (3+ qualifying children). The same tables list a $11,950 investment income limit for tax year 2025 and maximum AGI of $61,555 for filers with 3+ qualifying children (or $68,675 for married filing jointly).

items.title: IRS: Child Tax Credit up to $2,200 per qualifying child; refundable portion up to $1,700
items.summary: The IRS says the Child Tax Credit is up to $2,200 per qualifying child and the refundable Additional Child Tax Credit is up to $1,700 per qualifying child (subject to eligibility rules). The IRS also notes the refundable credit generally requires at least $2,500 in earned income and lists phaseout thresholds starting at $200,000 in income ($400,000 for joint filers).
